guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"e64bs","text":" Kit components ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":3,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6nc8h","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":6,"length":7},{"style":"bold","offset":23,"length":21},{"style":"bold","offset":54,"length":21},{"style":"bold","offset":85,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"7klh7","text":" General considerations To help prevent any carry-over DNA contamination, we recommend that separate areas be maintained for PCR set-up, PCR amplification and any post-PCR gel analysis. It is essential that any amplified PCR product should not be opened in the PCR set-up area. Primers: The sequence and concentration of the primers, as well as amplicon length, can be critical for specific amplification, yield and overall efficiency of any real-time PCR. We strongly recommend taking the following points into consideration when designing and running your real-time PCR: use primer-design software, such as Primer3 (http:// frodo.wi.mit.edu/primer3/) or visual OMPâ„˘ (http:// dnasoftware.com/). Primers should have a melting temperature (Tm) of approximately 60Â°C. optimal amplicon length should be 80-200 bp, and should not exceed 400 bp final primer concentration of 400 nM is suitable for most reactions, however to determine the optimal concentration we recommend titrating in the range 0.1-1 ÎĽM use an equimolar primer concentration Template: it is important that the DNA template is suitable for use in PCR in terms of purity and concentration. In addition, the template needs to be devoid of any contaminating PCR inhibitors (e.g. EDTA). The following should be considered when using genomic DNA templates: Genomic DNA: use up to 1 ÎĽg of complex (e.g. eukaryotic) genomic DNA in a single PCR. We recommend using the Bioline ISOLATE II Genomic DNA Mini Kit (BIO-52066) for high yield and purity from both prokaryotic and eukaryotic sources MgCl2: The MgCl2 concentration in the 1x reaction mix is 3 mM, which is optimal for SensiFAST HRM in the majority of real-time PCR conditions. If necessary, we suggest titrating MgCl2 to a maximum of 5 mM. PCR controls: It is important to detect the presence of contaminating DNA that may affect the reliability of the data. Always include a no template control (NTC), replacing the template with PCR-grade water. Troubleshooting Guide See the Bioline full documentation for detailed troubleshooting instructions. http://www.bioline.com/us/downloads/dl/file/id/2705/sensifast_hrm_kit_manual.pdf ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":6,"length":22},{"style":"bold","offset":291,"length":8},{"style":"bold","offset":1074,"length":9},{"style":"bold","offset":1352,"length":11},{"style":"bold","offset":1589,"length":6},{"style":"bold","offset":1799,"length":13},{"style":"bold","offset":2011,"length":21},{"style":"sub","offset":1593,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":1604,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":1771,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":636,"length":33},{"key":2,"offset":687,"length":24},{"key":3,"offset":2113,"length":80}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tables","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"cellsMeta":{"0_0":"","0_1":"","0_2":"","0_3":"","1_0":"","1_1":"","1_2":"","1_3":""},"colWidths":[null,null,null,null],"data":[["Reagent ","200 x 20 ÎĽL reactions ","500 x 20 ÎĽL reactions ","2000 x 20 ÎĽL reactions "],["SensiFAST HRM mix (2x) ","2 x 1 mL ","5 x 1 mL ","20 x 1 mL "]],"guid":"D0754EB0458011E9BAE4CF38B3347E4C","legend":"","mergeCells":[],"rowHeights":[],"show_headers":true}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"48EDE877430841F4B3EFC71687EE2C01","url":"http:// frodo.wi.mit.edu/primer3/"}},"2":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"54024B146201445AB7383C5FABD8B89E","url":"http:// dnasoftware.com/"}},"3":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"F3AAD86E8CA84EA1B3E1B0A2155F9354","url":"http://www.bioline.com/us/downloads/dl/file/id/2705/sensifast_hrm_kit_manual.pdf"}}}}
steps: Prepare a PCR master mix. The volumes given below are based on a standard 20 ÎĽL final reaction mix and can be scaled accordingly. %s %s

SensiFAST HRM Kit is compatible with either 3-step or 2-step cycling: 3 step cycle %s %s *2min for cDNA, 3min for genomic DNA ** Not recommended to extend beyond 20 seconds 2-step cycle %s %s *2min for cDNA, 3min for genomic DNA ** Not recommended to extend beyond 20 seconds
After the reaction has reached completion, refer to the instrument instructions for the option of melt-profile analysis.
